verdict: Simulate WhatsApp in-app with a pixel-faithful UI and document the Cloud API production path — do NOT attempt live integration for today's submission. Live Meta Cloud API via the free developer test number is technically reachable in ~1-3h (no business verification needed), but it is demo-poison: the test number can only exchange messages with up to 5 OTP-verified allowlisted phone numbers (judges can never message it), the temporary token expires every 24h (a deployed Vercel demo breaks overnight), and test-number templates don't carry to production. Twilio's WhatsApp sandbox is same-day usable but requires every participant to text a join code to a shared branded US number with 72h session expiry — worse theater than a clean simulator. SMS with a link is legally impossible today for a new entity: TRAI DLT requires entity (~₹5,900, 3-7 days) + header (1-2 days) + template (2-4 days) approval, and since Oct 1 2024 any URL in an SMS must additionally be whitelisted on the DLT portal or carriers block the message. The one genuinely live, zero-signup integration you CAN ship today: wa.me click-to-chat deep links (verified working) — a "Share via WhatsApp" button that opens real WhatsApp with prefilled advisory text.
demo_plan: 1) Build the WhatsApp simulator in-app (no external calls needed for the channel itself; Gemini answers server-side). Pixel-faithful spec: header bar #008069 (current WhatsApp green; legacy teal #075E54) with business avatar, name + verified badge, subtitle "Business Account", back arrow and video/call icons; chat wallpaper #EFEAE2 with faint doodle pattern (legacy #ECE5DD); yellow E2E-encryption notice chip at top ("Messages and calls are end-to-end encrypted"); centered date chip ("TODAY"); outgoing bubbles #D9FDD3 (legacy #DCF8C6) right-aligned with tail on first bubble of a group, incoming white #FFFFFF left-aligned; 11px gray timestamp bottom-right inside bubble followed by ticks (single gray = sent, double gray = delivered, double blue #53BDEB = read); voice-note bubble = play triangle + static waveform bars + duration + 1x/1.5x/2x speed pill + sender avatar with mic badge (green mic = unheard); quick replies rendered as full-width rounded buttons in WhatsApp link-blue (#00A5F4) below the bubble — cap at 3 buttons because the real Cloud API caps interactive reply buttons at 3 (verified in Meta docs), which makes the simulator provably API-faithful; input bar = rounded white pill (emoji, paperclip, camera) + green circular mic button that morphs to send arrow; "typing…" indicator under the business name. 2) Ship one REAL integration today: a "Get this on WhatsApp" button using the verified wa.me deep link — https://wa.me/<E164_no_plus>?text=<urlencoded advisory> (verified: 302 → api.whatsapp.com/send, opens real WhatsApp with prefilled text, zero signup, works on any judge's phone/desktop). 3) SMS: simulate a phone-frame SMS thread showing the advisory link; label it "SMS via DLT-registered header (production)" and show the DLT compliance path in the command center — do not send real SMS (Fast2SMS Quick SMS is ₹5/msg with per-message human approval and URL delivery is unreliable under TRAI whitelisting; Twilio international is $0.0832/SMS, replaces sender ID with a random shortcode, and prohibits shortened URLs to India). 4) In the docs/deck panel, print the real production endpoints (verified live: POST https://graph.facebook.com/v23.0/<PHONE_NUMBER_ID>/messages returns proper Graph JSON error without token) to prove the path is real.
production_plan: WhatsApp (primary channel): Meta WhatsApp Business Cloud API direct (free per-conversation pricing tiers; utility conversations cheap in India). Path: Meta developer app → WhatsApp product → dedicated business phone number → Meta Business verification (days to ~2 weeks) → display-name approval → System User permanent token (replaces 24h temp token) → webhook (POST from Meta) on a public HTTPS endpoint (Vercel route handler works). Send: POST https://graph.facebook.com/v23.0/<PHONE_NUMBER_ID>/messages with {messaging_product:"whatsapp", to, type, ...}; supports text, image, audio, documents, location and interactive reply buttons (max 3) / list messages. Inbound farmer media: webhook delivers message with media ID → GET https://graph.facebook.com/v23.0/<MEDIA_ID> returns {url, mime_type, id} (URL valid 5 min, media ID 7 days) → download with Bearer token → farmer voice notes arrive as audio/ogg OPUS (mono, ≤16MB) — feed straight to Gemini for STT/translation; crop photos arrive as image/jpeg. Business-initiated messages outside the 24h customer-service window require pre-approved templates. SMS (link-sharing channel): register on any one operator DLT portal (syncs across operators, e.g. Airtel/Jio/Vi) — entity ~₹5,900 one-time, 3-7 business days; header (sender ID like KISANV) 1-2 days; content templates 2-4 days; whitelist the kisanvaani domain/URL pattern as CTA (24-48h, free) per TRAI's Aug 2024 directive (enforced Oct 1 2024 — non-whitelisted URLs are carrier-blocked). Then send via an Indian aggregator (MSG91/Fast2SMS/Exotel, roughly ₹0.15-0.30/SMS transactional) with DLT entity ID + template ID on every API call. Total realistic lead time to first compliant SMS with a link: ~1-2 weeks. Avoid Twilio for India domestic SMS (sender ID replaced, $0.0832/msg, shortened URLs prohibited).

findings.source_name: Meta WhatsApp Cloud API — Get Started
findings.verification_note: Fetched doc page. Confirms: free Meta dev account + app with WhatsApp product is enough to start; no business verification for testing; example endpoint https://graph.facebook.com/v23.0/<TEST_BUSINESS_PHONE_NUMBER_ID>/messages; webhooks deliver incoming messages and statuses.
findings.notes: Free test number is provisioned in the app dashboard's API Setup panel.

findings.source_name: Meta Graph API messages endpoint (liveness probe)
findings.verification_note: curl POST without token returned live Graph JSON error {error:{type:GraphMethodException,code:100,error_subcode:33,fbtrace_id:...}} — endpoint alive and responding; requires Bearer access token for real sends.
findings.notes: Payload fields: messaging_product:'whatsapp', to, type, plus type-specific object.

findings.source_name: Meta Cloud API — Send Messages reference
findings.verification_note: Fetched. Supports text, image, audio, video, document, location, interactive reply buttons ('up to three predefined replies') and interactive lists within the 24h service window; JSON fields messaging_product/recipient_type/to/type confirmed.
findings.notes: The 3-button cap is the number to replicate in the simulator's quick replies for API fidelity.

findings.source_name: Meta Cloud API — Media reference (inbound voice notes/images)
findings.verification_note: Fetched. GET https://graph.facebook.com/<VER>/<MEDIA_ID> returns {url, mime_type, id}; media URL expires in 5 minutes, media ID downloadable for 7 days; download requires Authorization header; voice notes are audio/ogg 'OPUS codecs only, mono input only', 16MB cap.
findings.notes: Confirms farmer voice notes + crop photos are fully receivable via webhook → media fetch → Gemini pipeline.

findings.source_name: Meta test phone number limitations (WANotifier helpdesk)
findings.verification_note: Fetched. Test number can only message recipients pre-added in the Meta portal; arbitrary customers cannot message the test number; templates created on test number don't carry to production. Corroborated by search: max 5 recipients, each OTP-verified, temp token expires every 24h.
findings.notes: This is why live integration fails as a judged demo: judges' phones can never talk to the test number.

findings.source_name: Meta webhooks / echo-bot test endpoint doc
findings.verification_note: Found via search only, not fetched this session. Meta's own docs include an echo-bot walkthrough, confirming inbound messages from allowlisted recipients DO reach webhooks in test setups (message types include text, image, audio).
findings.notes: Inbound works for the 5 allowlisted numbers, contradicting a broad reading of the WANotifier 'no inbound' claim — inbound is blocked only for non-allowlisted numbers.

findings.verification_note: Fetched. Same-day activation with just a Twilio account: users text 'join <sandbox-code>' to +14155238886; sessions expire after 3 days; rate limit 1 msg/3s; only 3 fixed pre-approved templates; sandbox number carries Twilio branding.
findings.notes: Rejected for demo: join-code friction, shared branded US number, 72h expiry. Fine as a private dev testbed only.

findings.verification_note: Fetched. Inbound webhook POST includes NumMedia, MediaUrl{N}, MediaContentType{N}, plus WhatsApp-specific ProfileName, WaId, Forwarded, FrequentlyForwarded — so images/voice notes arrive as fetchable media URLs.
findings.notes: Voice notes arrive with audio content type via MediaUrl0; form-encoded POST.

findings.verification_note: Fetched. Domestic India traffic requires DLT company + sender ID registration before Twilio provisioning (alphanumeric ~10 business days); international route needs no DLT but sender ID is replaced with a random 5-9 digit shortcode; shortened URLs are prohibited content for India.
findings.notes: Kills Twilio as a today-option for branded link SMS to Indian farmers.

findings.verification_note: Fetched. Outbound SMS to India from international numbers: $0.0832 per message (~Rs 7).
findings.notes: ~25-50x the cost of DLT-registered Indian aggregator routes (~Rs 0.15-0.30).

findings.verification_note: Fetched. From Oct 1 2024 carriers block bulk SMS containing any URL/APK/OTT link not whitelisted by the sender on a DLT portal; whitelisting on one operator syncs to all; approval 24-48 working hours. Primary source PIB press release PRID=2058943 returned 403 to automated fetch (exists per search).
findings.notes: Even a DLT-registered sender cannot send the kisanvaani.app link until the URL itself is whitelisted — cite this as why the demo simulates SMS.

findings.verification_note: Fetched. Quick SMS route sends without DLT at Rs 5/SMS with per-message manual human approval by Fast2SMS staff; delivers to DND and non-DND; URL policy not stated on page.
findings.notes: Rejected: manual approval latency makes it undemoable live, Rs 5/msg, and URL delivery is unreliable post-TRAI-whitelisting. Their /dlt page (also fetched) confirms DLT is otherwise mandatory.

findings.source_name: DLT registration costs & timelines (2Factor/MSG91 aggregator guidance)
findings.verification_note: From search results only (msg91.com/help DLT page 404'd on old URL). Consistent figures across sources: entity registration ~Rs 5,900 (Rs 5,000+GST) one-time per operator portal, entity approval 3-7 business days, header 1-2 days, content template 2-4 business days.
findings.notes: Use as the production-timeline citation: ~1-2 weeks from zero to first compliant link-bearing SMS.

findings.verification_note: curl verified this session: https://wa.me/919999999999?text=Namaste%20KisanVaani returned 302 → https://api.whatsapp.com/send/?phone=...&text=... and api.whatsapp.com/send returned 200. No signup, no API key, opens real WhatsApp with prefilled message.
findings.notes: The one live WhatsApp integration shippable today: 'Get this advisory on WhatsApp' share button in the deployed demo.

findings.verification_note: Not fetched; spec compiled from well-established public values: header #008069 (legacy #075E54), brand green #25D366, wallpaper #EFEAE2/#ECE5DD, outgoing bubble #D9FDD3/#DCF8C6, read ticks #53BDEB. The 3-button quick-reply cap IS verified against fetched Meta send-messages docs.
findings.notes: Full component spec (ticks, voice-note player with speed toggle, E2E chip, business header, input pill) is in embed_data.whatsappUiSpec.
